book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

title: Chapter IL.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But in addition to all that has been said already, I wish to adduce still further proof, so that all may understand what impiety is contained in this assertion of yours.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For if your allegation is true, that He was not born, then it will follow undoubtedly that He did not suffer; for it is not possible for one to suffer who was not also born.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But if He did not suffer, then the name of the cross is done away with.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And if the cross was not endured, then Jesus did not rise from the dead.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And if Jesus rose not from the dead, then no other person will rise again.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And if no one shall rise again, then there will be no judgment.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For it is certain that, if I am not to rise again, I cannot be judged.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But if there is to be no judgment, then the keeping of God’s commandments will be to no purpose, and there will be no occasion for abstinence: nay, we may say, “Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we shall die.”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For all these consequences follow when you deny that He was born of Mary.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But if you acknowledge that He was born of Mary, then His passion will necessarily follow, and His resurrection will be consequent on His passion, and the judgment on His resurrection: and thus the injunctions of Scripture will have their proper value for us.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: This is not therefore an idle question, but there are the mightiest issues involved in this word.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For just as all the law and the prophets are summed up in two words, so also all our hope is made to depend on the birth by the blessed Mary.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Give me therefore an answer to these several questions which I shall address to you.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: How shall we get rid of these many words of the apostle, so important and so precise, which are expressed in terms like the following: “But when the good pleasure of God was with us, He sent His Son, made of a woman;” and again, “Christ our passover is sacrificed for us;” and once more, “God hath both raised up the Lord, and will raise up us together with Him by His own power?”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And there are many other passages of a similar import; as, for example, this which follows: “How say some among you, that there is no resurrection of the dead?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is not Christ risen: and if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vain.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Yea, and we shall be found false witnesses of God; who have testified against God that He raised up Christ: whom He raised not up.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ risen: and if Christ be not raised, your faith is vain; ye are yet in your sins.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are perished.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: If in this life only we have hope in Christ, we are more miserable than all men.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: But now is Christ risen from the dead, the beginning of them that sleep;” and so on.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Who, then, I ask, can be found so rash and audacious as not to make his faith fit in with these sacred words, in which there is no qualification nor any dubiety?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Who, I ask you, O foolish Galatian, has bewitched you, as those were bewitched “before whose eyes Jesus Christ was evidently set forth, crucified?”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: From all this I think that these testimonies should suffice in proof of the judgment, and the resurrection, and the passion; and the birth by Mary is also shown to be involved naturally and at once in these facts.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And what matters it though you refuse to acquiesce in this, when the Scripture proclaims the fact most unmistakeably?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Nevertheless I shall again put a question to you, and let it please you to give me an answer.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: When Jesus gave His testimony concerning John, and said, “Among them that are born of women there hath not risen a greater than John the Baptist: notwithstanding, he that is less in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he,” tell me what is meant by there being a greater than he in the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Was Jesus less in the kingdom of heaven than John?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: I say, God forbid!

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Tell me, then, how this is to be explained, and you will certainly surpass yourself.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Without doubt the meaning is, that Jesus was less than John among those that are born of woman; but in the kingdom of heaven He is greater than he.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Wherefore tell me this too, O Manichæus: If you say that Christ was not born of Mary, but that He only appeared like a man, while yet He was not really a man, the appearance being effected and produced by the power that is in Him, tell me, I repeat, on whom then was it that the Spirit descended like a dove?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Who is this that was baptized by John?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: If He was perfect, if He was the Son, if He was the Power, the Spirit could not have entered into Him; just as a kingdom cannot enter within a kingdom.

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: And whose, too, was that voice which was sent forth out of heaven, and which gave Him this testimony, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased?”

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Come, tell me; make no delay; who is this that acquires all these things, that does all these things?

verses.book: The Acts of the Disputation with the Heresiarch Manes.
verses.text: Answer me: Will you thus audaciously adduce blasphemy for reason, and will you attempt to find a place for it?
